drill.title: Final Third Possession in a 2-3-1 Shape

notes: Clarity Failure (Age Mismatch): The drill is specified for `ageGroup: "U10"` but repeatedly references "U12" in the `organization` and `loadNotes` sections. The tactical complexity is better suited for U12s.

Clarity Failure (Inconsistent Numbers): Player numbers are contradictory across multiple fields: `organization` (4v3+GK), `description` (4v4+GK), `diagram.players` (4 attackers vs 4 defenders + GK), and `diagram.teams` (4 attack vs 3 defend).

Structural Failure (Malformed `organization`): The `organization` key is a string instead of the required object. It is missing critical sub-keys like `setupSteps`, `rotation`, `restarts`, and `scoring`, making the drill impossible to set up clearly.
Structural Failure (Duplicate Keys): The JSON contains both `progressions` (the correct array) and `progression` (a forbidden duplicate key), which violates the required data structure.
Realism Concern: The tactical concepts, such as timed third-man runs and specific cutback patterns, are generally too advanced for a U10 group, impacting the drill's realism for that age.
Load Concern: 90-second work intervals, while paired with a 1:1 rest, may be too long for U10s, potentially leading to a drop-off in decision-making quality and physical execution.
qa.summary: The drill has a solid conceptual foundation with good constraints and a clear psychological theme, but it fails on execution due to critical structural and clarity issues. A severe age mismatch (U10 vs U12), wildly inconsistent player counts, and malformed/duplicate JSON keys render it unusable without a complete rewrite.

json.setup: Set up 4v3+GK in Width of the 18-yard box to the halfway line (~44x35yd for U12). Coach at midfield with balls. Mark a wide channel (for winger isolation) and a cutback zone. Start from CAM on coach restart.
json.title: Final Third Possession in a 2-3-1 Shape
json.topic: Maintaining Possession to Create Scoring Chances

coach.restart: Coach plays into CAM to start each 90s rep

loadNotes.rationale: Short work intervals preserve decision quality and avoid excessive fatigue for U12.
loadNotes.structure: 8 x 90s with 90s rest (1:1) — rotate groups briskly

roleUsage.notes: The focus is on the attacking unit from the 2-3-1 formation learning to combine in the final third.

teamShape.notes: The attacking team replicates the roles and spacing of the front four players in a 2-3-1 formation to build familiarity with attacking patterns.

constraints: Controlled start: Defenders cannot press beyond the CAM’s starting line until after the CAM’s first touch.
Attackers must complete a minimum of 3 passes before shooting.
A goal scored from a cross is worth 2 points.
Defenders have 8 seconds to score in the mini-goals after winning possession.
json.description: A 4v4+GK game where attackers, set up in the shape of a 2-3-1's front four, work to maintain possession and score on a large goal. The defending team aims to win the ball and counter-attack to two mini-goals.
progression: Coaching challenge: Encourage third-man runs with guided questions and freeze moments.
Add one recovering defender from halfway on coach signal → creates 4v4 momentary transition.
json.organization: Format: 4v3+GK (U12). Area: Width of the 18-yard box to the halfway line (~44x35yd for U12). Objective: POSSESSION principles (switch → third-man timing → cutback). Rotation via 90s bouts (1:1 rest).
progressions: Remove the 3-pass minimum to encourage quicker attacks and decision-making.
Introduce a 'one-touch finish' rule for double points to reward good movement and early shots.
Add two neutral players on the sidelines who play for the team in possession, creating a 6v4 overload for the attackers to focus on ball circulation.
scoringHints: +1 bonus point for a goal scored by a third-man runner (e.g., CAM timing beyond the 9).
coachingPoints: Maintain a good attacking shape with width and depth to stretch the defense.
Scan the field before receiving the ball to know your next move.
Use intelligent movement off the ball to create passing lanes for your teammates.
Be decisive in the final third: recognize when to pass, dribble, or shoot.
React quickly in transition: if possession is lost, press immediately to win it back.
GK: starting position & communication on cutbacks; quick distribution on saves.

coach.label: Coach
coach.note: Coach plays into CAM to start each 90s rep
